meta.next_session: 2026-07-01 6:00 AM PDT (Daily Brief) — archive today's AH report; pre-open gap check; route any new deposit to DRIP (most underweight, -6.4pp); SAFE MODE persists until a fresh matrix is computed

thoughts.label: DRIP SLEEVE OPENED — JEPQ $36 SEED
thoughts.text: Reconciliation surfaced a NEW holding vs this morning's memory: JEPQ 0.5878 sh @ $61.25 ($36.00), an agentic market buy filled 08:26 PDT today that an intervening session executed but never wrote to memory. Per §5.1 the broker is truth — JEPQ is now a held DRIP position. This converts the $36.37 idle cash into the income sleeve, lifting DRIP from 0% to 23.6% and finally clearing the standing top-capital-priority flag. No deposit involved (cash -> ETF reallocation, TWR-invisible).

thoughts.label: RECONCILED — 7 POSITIONS, NO STOP BREACHES
thoughts.text: Broker now holds 3 LAB (INTC, ARKK, NVDA) + 3 CORE (VOO, QQQ, SOXX) + 1 DRIP (JEPQ) = 7. LAB gap/stop check CLEAR: INTC +24.5%, ARKK +10.1%, NVDA +5.1% above their ATR stops. Cash $0.37. CORE/DRIP carry no stops (§13/§15.4).

thoughts.label: SAFE MODE — 3:20 PM ENGINE RUN DID NOT FIRE
thoughts.text: The scheduled 3:20 PM close+AH Signal Engine run did not fire. The latest signal_matrix.json is the 12:21 PDT run, 3h12m old at this 3:33 PM session — over the §2.5 3-hour limit. CLU is in SAFE MODE: no new entries, manage existing positions only. The 3:20 PM miss is the recurring engine-reliability flag; SAFE MODE has now recurred across multiple sessions.

thoughts.label: STRONG TECH TAPE, NEUTRAL COMPOSITES
thoughts.text: Best quarter for the S&P/Nasdaq in six years closed today. Holdings ripped: INTC +5.9%, SOXX +4.2%, NVDA +2.5%, QQQ +1.6%. Yet composites stay NEUTRAL/BEARISH (-0.06 to -0.34) because MACD remains Bearish on most names — the histogram lags the V-recovery. Price leads, the quant blend hasn't confirmed.

thoughts.label: DRIP STILL THE ROUTING TARGET FOR NEW CASH
thoughts.text: After the JEPQ seed, allocation is LAB 25.9% / CORE 50.2% / DRIP 23.6% / cash 0.2% vs Accumulation targets 20/50/30. DRIP remains the most-underweight tier (-6.4pp) and the next-deposit target (tiebreak DRIP>CORE>LAB). With only $0.37 cash there is nothing to route now; wait for the next deposit. Dividend engine still emits streak=0/payout=null, so the §15.3 screen stays unverifiable — prefer covered-call JEPI/JEPQ (payout N/A by rule) until streak/payout populate.

thoughts.label: LAB OVER STAGING CAP — NO NEW SINGLE-STOCK ENTRIES
thoughts.text: LAB is 25.9% vs its 20% Accumulation cap. NET now shows the cleanest gate (4/5) and RXRX holds 3/5, but §17.3 forbids force-selling to rebalance, so no LAB add is authorized; the sleeve dilutes via base growth. Combined with SAFE MODE and ~$0 cash, no LAB entry is on the table.

learned_patterns.pattern: An agentic JEPQ buy ($36, 08:26 PDT) filled but was never written to memory by the session that placed it — surfaced only by the §3 broker reconciliation. Confirms the discipline: broker state is the source of truth (§5.1), prose memory lags. Untracked agentic fills will appear and must be folded in, not undone (DRIP is never sold on signal).

The Signal Engine again missed its 3:20 PM close+AH run; the AH session ran on the 12:21 PDT matrix (3h12m stale), forcing SAFE MODE per §2.5. The guard keeps working, but recurring scheduled misses mean back-to-back sessions cannot act on signal. Workflow cron/runner reliability remains the top operational fix.

Best quarter in six years closed with holdings up sharply (INTC +5.9%, SOXX +4.2%, QQQ +1.6%) while composites stayed NEUTRAL/BEARISH (-0.06 to -0.34). MACD histograms remain Bearish on most names — the signed-momentum term lags a fast V-recovery, so the gate reads neutral even as price rips. Composite is a lagging confirmation in sharp reversals, not a leading one.

The DRIP seed (cash->JEPQ) moved the book from 0% DRIP to a near-target 26/50/24 LAB/CORE/DRIP mix in one $36 trade — deposit-free reallocation is the fastest path to the staging glide path when a tier sits at 0%. LAB still 25.9% > 20% cap; it dilutes only via base growth (§17.3), not trimming.

NET emerged as the cleanest LAB gate (4/5) purely on a low Z (0.48) + Bullish MACD, while RXRX sits exactly on the Z=1.50 line (fails by the >=1.5 boundary) and CRWD fell to 2/5 on an RSI-80 overbought print. Gate ranking reshuffles session to session on small Z/RSI moves — re-rank every session, never carry a stale top-3.

Dividend yields now render sanely (JEPQ 9.95%, JEPI 8.10%, SCHD 3.30%) but dividend_growth_streak=0 and payout_ratio=null persist for every DRIP name, so the §15.3 screen (streak>=5, payout<80%) stays unverifiable. Covered-call names (JEPI/JEPQ, payout N/A by rule) can seed on yield alone; dividend-growth names (SCHD/DGRO/VIG/VYM) wait on the streak/payout fix.
